heading: Quality assurance program
label: 892b Quality assurance program
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a). substituted “product or service produced or disseminated” for “product produced”.Pub. L. 117–2632002—Subsec. (b)(1). reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Administrator may—Pub. L. 107–372“(A) develop and implement a quality assurance program that is equally available to all applicants, under which the Administrator may certify hydrographic products that satisfy the standards promulgated by the Administrator under ;section 892a(a)(3) of this title“(B) authorize the use of the emblem or any trademark of the Administration on a hydrographic product certified under subparagraph (A); and“(C) charge a fee for such certification and use.”
amendments: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a). substituted “product or service produced or disseminated” for “product produced”.Pub. L. 117–2632002—Subsec. (b)(1). reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Administrator may—Pub. L. 107–372“(A) develop and implement a quality assurance program that is equally available to all applicants, under which the Administrator may certify hydrographic products that satisfy the standards promulgated by the Administrator under ;section 892a(a)(3) of this title“(B) authorize the use of the emblem or any trademark of the Administration on a hydrographic product certified under subparagraph (A); and“(C) charge a fee for such certification and use.”

children.heading: Definition
children.label: a Definition
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “hydrographic product” means any publicly or commercially available product or service produced or disseminated by a non-Federal entity that includes or displays hydrographic data.

children.label: A
children.text: by not later than 2 years after , shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, develop and implement a quality assurance program that is equally available to all applicants, under which the Administrator may certify hydrographic products that satisfy the standards promulgated by the Administrator under ;December 19, 2002section 892a(a)(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: may authorize the use of the emblem or any trademark of the Administration on a hydrographic product certified under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: may charge a fee for such certification and use.

children.heading: Limitation on fee amount
children.label: 2 Limitation on fee amount
children.text: Any fee under paragraph (1)(C) shall not exceed the costs of conducting the quality assurance testing, evaluation, or studies necessary to determine whether the hydrographic product satisfies the standards adopted under , including the cost of administering such a program.section 892a(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on liability
children.label: c Limitation on liability
children.text: The Government of the United States shall not be liable for any negligence by a person that produces hydrographic products certified under this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury a separate account, which shall be known as the “Hydrographic Services Account”.

children.label: A
children.text: amounts received by the United States as fees charged under subsection (b)(1)(C); and

children.label: B
children.text: such other amounts as may be provided by law.

children.heading: Use
children.label: 3 Use
children.text: Amounts in the account shall be available to the Administrator, without further appropriation, for hydrographic services.

children.heading: Limitation on new fees and increases in existing fees for hydrographic services
children.label: e Limitation on new fees and increases in existing fees for hydrographic services

children.text: establish any fee or other charge for the provision of any hydrographic service except as authorized by this section; or
increase the amount of any fee or other charge for the provision of any hydrographic service except as authorized by this section and .section 1307 of title 44
